fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 34-year-old Hispanic female with schizoaffective disorder for routine follow-up.
fields.Subjective: Patient presented appearing calm and well-engaged. Reports taking her lurasidone 120 mg with dinner every evening as prescribed, using phone reminder that alerts her. States she is doing quite well overall. Experiences very infrequent brief auditory hallucinations maybe once every couple weeks - just hears someone call her name - which she dismisses easily. No other psychotic symptoms. Sleep excellent at 8 hours nightly. Appetite good, weight stable at 157 lbs. Working as administrative assistant 30 hours weekly, performing job well. Mood 'good, content with life' rated 8/10. Lives with mother which provides stability and support. Enjoys crafting and sells handmade jewelry online as hobby. Attends church regularly. Engaged in weekly therapy working on assertiveness skills. No substance use. Socially connected through church community and craft groups. No safety concerns, denies SI/HI. (Seen by Dr. Daniel Kim, MD, Psychiatrist)

Mental_Status_Examination.Appearance: Casually dressed, good grooming, relaxed manner
Mental_Status_Examination.Thought_Content: Minimal residual symptoms with good insight

fields.Current_Medications: Lurasidone 120 mg PO daily with food

Laboratory_Data.Recent_Lab_Results: Metabolic panel normal; lipids within acceptable range.

fields.Diagnostic_Impression: Schizoaffective Disorder, stable with minimal symptoms.
fields.Psychosocial_Context: Lives with mother; employed part-time; engaged in therapy and hobbies; active church involvement; good functional status.
Assessment_and_Plan: Excellent medication adherence with phone reminder system.
Continue lurasidone 120 mg daily with food.
fields.Author: Note written by Dr. Daniel Kim, MD, Psychiatrist — Horizon Mental Wellness, 2789 Sunset Boulevard, Phoenix, AZ 85013, Phone: (555) 728-3341, Fax: (555) 728-3342
